claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me quickly review the official eligibility criteria so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 21 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I can see that Alex is 21 years old and lives at home with you.

To assess eligibility, I need to understand Alex's current situation. **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training, but it typically stops when they turn 20.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any approved education or training. He actually works full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me explain the situation: According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 **and** they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 21 years old, he is over the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even though he lives with you. Child Benefit stops when a young person turns 20, or earlier if they leave approved education or training before that age.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The reason is that Alex is 21 years old, which is above the maximum age limit. Child Benefit stops when a young person turns 20, even if they continue living at home. Since Alex is also working full-time rather than being in approved education or training, this confirms he is beyond the eligibility criteria.

I hope this clarifies your situation. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of whether he lives at home or is in education/training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and works full-time. As Alex is over the age of 20, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit can only continue beyond age 16 if the young person is under 20 and in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of whether he lives at home or is in education/training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and works full-time. As Alex is over the age of 20, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Child Benefit can only continue beyond age 16 if the young person is under 20 and in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
